career_research_cornell.title: Career Research Cornell Notes
career_research_cornell.subtitle: Sort facts, questions, and personal fit
career_research_cornell.learning_target: I can collect career information and summarize what matters most.
career_research_cornell.directions: Put categories and questions in the cue column. Put facts, examples, and useful details in the notes column.
career_research_cornell.summary_prompt: Would I learn more about this pathway? Why?
career_research_cornell.reflection_prompt: What question still needs an answer?

guest_speaker_guided_notes.title: Guest Speaker Guided Notes
guest_speaker_guided_notes.subtitle: Daily work, skills, challenges, and advice
guest_speaker_guided_notes.learning_target: I can listen for career details and connect them to my own strengths or questions.
guest_speaker_guided_notes.directions: Write short notes during the presentation, video profile, or interview. Focus on details you could use later.
prompts: What work or pathway is being described?
What happens during a typical day?
What connects to my strengths or interests?
success_criteria: I wrote at least one useful question.
I connected the pathway to myself honestly.

pathway_comparison_notes.title: Pathway Comparison Notes
pathway_comparison_notes.subtitle: Facts on one side, personal fit on the other
pathway_comparison_notes.learning_target: I can compare pathway information and explain what fits me.
pathway_comparison_notes.directions: Put career facts on the left. Put reactions, connections, or questions on the right.
pathway_comparison_notes.summary_prompt: Which pathway should I explore next?
